name: 21_Iris_Classification_SVM_Iris_ML
query: I request a project to classify iris species utilizing the Iris dataset with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier implemented in `src/model.py`. The project should standardize the data in and perform feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`. It will document the classification accuracy and save it as `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt`, and generate and save a confusion matrix as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. It will further create an interactive web application in `src/app.py` using Streamlit to showcase classification results and model performance, with the figures stored in `results/figures/`. The web page should be user-friendly, with a brief explanation of the model to help users understand how the SVM classifier works.

requirements.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.
A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

preferences.criteria: The Streamlit web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily explore different aspects of the model's performance.
A brief model explanation should be included on the web page, helping users understand how the SVM classifier works.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code in `src/data_loader.py` is a minimal semantic placeholder that does not demonstrate actual loading or use of the Iris dataset. The `load_dataset()` function returns a generic dictionary with no reference to the Iris dataset, and there is no evidence in the file content of loading from `iris.csv` or any other source specific to the Iris dataset. The trajectory evidence mentions loading `iris.csv` in Step 5, but this is only referenced as environment feedback and is not reflected in the actual code provided for evaluation.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code in `src/data_loader.py` does not contain any implementation for data standardization. The file only includes placeholder functions (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and requirement documentation, with no actual code that scales or normalizes feature values to ensure they are within the same range. The trajectory evidence references `StandardScaler` usage, but this is not reflected in the actual code content provided.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code in `src/data_loader.py` does not contain any actual feature selection implementation. The file only includes placeholder functions (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and requirement documentation. There is no code that performs feature selection methods (e.g., variance threshold, correlation analysis, model-based selection) to identify important features. The trajectory evidence mentions that feature selection was performed in execution, but the code file itself lacks the corresponding implementation, failing to meet the requirement as specified for the file.

<UNSATISFIED>
The provided code in `src/model.py` does not implement an SVM classifier. The file contains only a placeholder function `build_model()` that returns a string "model placeholder" and includes some metadata, but there is no actual SVM implementation using libraries like scikit-learn or custom code. The requirement explicitly calls for the SVM classifier to be implemented in this file, which is not present.

<UNSATISFIED>
The file `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which does not contain a valid classification accuracy value. The requirement expects the saved content to be the actual classification accuracy, not a placeholder or tuple representation.

judge_stats.criteria: A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided evidence shows an error when attempting to read the `confusion_matrix.png` file, indicating it was not successfully generated or saved. The error message states "No endpoints found that support image input," which, while related to the reading method, confirms the file's content cannot be verified. The trajectory mentions the file was created, but the direct file reading result is an error, failing to satisfy the requirement of a generated and saved confusion matrix.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>
The provided `src/app.py` file contains only a minimal semantic implementation with placeholder functions and hardcoded return values. It does not create an actual interactive Streamlit web application that loads and displays classification results or model performance figures from `results/figures/`. The code lacks Streamlit components (e.g., `st.title()`, `st.image()`, `st.write()`) and file loading logic to showcase the required content.
